title: API
description: HTTP/OpenAPI reference for the you-agent-factory API: published operations, local-server base URL, and static-only docs with no live request playground.
openingSummary: The you-agent-factory API is the HTTP contract a running factory host exposes. The published OpenAPI (Open Application Programming Interface) surface lists operations, request and response shapes, and the local-server base URL for that contract—without treating the documentation host as the live API.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: The you-agent-factory HTTP/OpenAPI surface lists the published operations a factory host exposes, how those operations are grouped and addressed, and the local-server base URL callers use against a running host. Use it for the OpenAPI projection rather than a CLI matrix, MCP host setup, or a hand-authored orientation outline.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: OpenAPI (Open Application Programming Interface) describes the HTTP API a running factory host exposes. An operation is one method-and-path pair with a stable operation id. Tags group related operations for navigation. The local-server base URL comes from the OpenAPI servers list and names where callers send requests—typically a local factory host—not the documentation site. The published API docs are static: they do not run a request playground or require a reachable factory host to render. Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream operations use HTTP transport summaries here and link toward the events reference for the event catalog.

operations.title: Operations
operations.body: The published OpenAPI projection lists every HTTP operation on one searchable page. Use tag-grouped navigation and filtering to move between endpoints; each operation section keeps a stable hash anchor for deep links.
